non_blocking_risks.risk: If governance-checkpoints change after feedback hooks are defined, the feedback re-entry target may need to be remapped.

same_source_consistency.evidence: The demand-visibility, open-hypothesis, review, and feedback artifacts all point to the same unresolved demand-formation object.

cross_format_consistency.evidence: The discovery outputs and governance outputs refer to the same reviewable demand assumptions rather than two different objects.

fact_validation_chain.item: Confirm latent demand and visibility barriers exist in demand-visibility.json.
Confirm open assumptions and reversible pruning exist in hypothesis-register.json.
Confirm feedback re-entry points are defined in feedback-hooks.json.
Confirm governance-checkpoints and feedback-ledger can consume those hooks without redefining discovery outputs.

unverifiable_assumptions.item: The real-world feedback source will produce evidence at the granularity assumed by feedback-hooks.json.

scope_alignment_checks.item: No runtime governance operations artifact was required; the round stays on human review and writeback boundaries.

check.note: demand visibility and open-hypothesis artifacts were created at the correct upstream stage.
governance-checkpoints and feedback-ledger were added before finish and after discovery hooks were defined.

dispatch_recommendations.item: Proceed because discovery owns latent-demand framing and governance owns actual review and feedback application without hidden overlap.

check.why: It is the bridge between demand discovery and later accountable feedback application.
check.evidence: feedback-ledger.json and governance-checkpoints.json depend on it conceptually.

check.why: Without it, there is no explicit human review boundary for the feedback writeback path.
check.evidence: mode-resolution.json marks governance as the final authority for feedback application.

evaluation_report.score_reason: The example cleanly demonstrates discovery-governance handoff, but still covers only one discovery-heavy interaction pattern.
